payload.answer: The proposed update appears to be an oversized table-to-memory conversion. I can offer repair options, but I will not commit 500 separate memory cards as-is.
repair_options: Reject the bulk import and ask the user to identify which rows contain durable personal memory.
Summarize the CSV into a smaller number of thematic memory candidates for user review instead of one memory per row.
Import only rows that contain durable, user-specific preferences, facts, or commitments; discard transient, generic, or non-memory rows.
Ask the user to confirm whether the CSV is intended as reference data rather than personal memory.
If the rows are independent durable facts, offer to batch them into smaller review groups before any saving occurs.
If many rows describe the same subject, offer to consolidate them into a single candidate memory with preserved qualifiers and ambiguity.
Keep the CSV external and store only a pointer or note about where it can be found, if the user wants recall of the dataset location.
Ask the user to provide filtering criteria, such as columns to include, date relevance, entity scope, or minimum importance threshold.
